log_entries.thought_preview: The sample is a PE32 executable, likely a spreader with an overlay, and is available in two sandboxes: CAPA and Dr.Web vxCube.
The sample dropped a large number of files, but did not create any network connections, registry persistence keys, or mutexes.
The sample created a single process, but did not load any notable modules or create mutexes, and did not make any notable API calls.
The sample dropped a large number of files, many of which are registry files (.reg) and batch files (.bat), suggesting a possible macro or registry-based infection.
The sample did not create any registry persistence keys, which suggests that it may not be using a traditional registry-based persistence mechanism.
The CAPA sandbox detected 4 MITRE ATT&CK techniques and 9 signature matches, but did not capture any network traffic or memory dumps.
The sample did not establish any network connections, DNS lookups, or HTTP conversations, which suggests that it may be a local-only infection.
The sample exhibits behaviors consistent with file system interaction, such as getting file attributes, writing files, and enumerating GUI resources.
